rulings.Case Title: NIGEL CHANTER ET AL VS. GREGORY MALIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 22, 2024 line 1. PETITIONER NIGEL CHANTER, LAI CHEUNG, PETER BAUMANNN, ALISON BAUMANN, TON-YUN FANG, GNOMES COVE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, BABAC DOANE Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment is OFF CALENDAR. Amended Notice of Motion on file. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BARRETT DAFFIN FRAPPIER TREDER & WEISS, LLP VS. SHARON DRUMMOND ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 22, 2024 line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT CITIBANK, N.A., SUCCESSOR BY MERGER TO CITIBANK (WEST), FSB DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Cross-Complainants to properly state their causes of action. Each cause of action shall be separately labeled and each element of each cause of action must be supported by facts. Conclusory allegations are not sufficient. Cross-Complainants shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended Cross-Complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. CHRISTINA DE LUCA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 22, 2024 line 7. PLAINTIFF THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion is GRANTED. Moving party shifted their burden and opposing party failed to create triable issues of fact with competent admissible evidence. Motion for summary judgment is framed by the pleadings. The property filed operative pleadings on file are the Complaint and September 6, 2024 Answer (in conjunction with October 16, 2024 Order). Defendant's November 12, 2024 Amended Answer filed late and without leave of Court is stricken. Even if the Court were to consider the Amended Answer, it would not be sufficient to deny Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment as Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that their Amended Answer maybe submitted in lieu of evidence in opposition to the motion for summary judgment. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 634 POWELL I5, LP VS. NICKLAUS ODA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 22, 2024 line 9. DEFENDANT NICKLAUS ODA, DONNA LITTLE DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The Court notes that the moving party cited to the 1971 Second Edition of Witkin in support of its argument. Defendant did not provide a copy of this outdated secondary source. The current edition of Witkin is the 6th edition, which states that lack of legal capacity to sue is seldom available as ground for demurrer as "the plaintiff need not allege the capacity to sue." The court further notes that the sole listed ground for demurrer in the Notice of demurrer is that the complaint "fails to state a cause of action." The special demurrer under CCP 430.10(b) is not mentioned in the Notice of Demurrer. Defendant fails to show that lack of capacity is disclosed on the face of the complaint. Defendant provides an incomplete quote of B&P 17900(b)(2) omitting the first part of this section. Nothing on the face of the complaint discloses that Plaintiff does not satisfy the requirements of the first part of B&P 17900(b)(2). The Court further notes that Plaintiff attempts to improperly introduce extrinsic facts by attaching documents as exhibits directly to the points and authorities. Counsel for both parties are ordered to familiarize themselves with the Code of Civil Procedure and California Rules of Court on proper format of the motions and submission of evidence. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
